docstring.text:
Prompt converters for transforming prompts before sending them to targets in red teaming workflows.

Converters are organized into categories: Text-to-Text (encoding, obfuscation, translation, variation),
Audio (text-to-audio, audio-to-text, audio-to-audio), Image (text-to-image, image-to-image),
Video (image-to-video), File (text-to-PDF/URL), Selective Converting (partial prompt transformation),
and Human-in-the-Loop (interactive review). Converters can be stacked together to create complex
transformation pipelines for testing AI system robustness.

Adds a string to an image and wraps the text into multiple lines if necessary.

This class is similar to :class:`AddTextImageConverter` except
we pass in an image file path as an argument to the constructor as opposed to text.
Convert the given prompt by adding it as text to the image.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: The text to be added to the image.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: The type of input data.

returns.desc: The result containing path to the updated image.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported.

docstring.text: Initialize the converter with the image file path and text properties.

params.name: img_to_add
params.desc: File path of image to add text to.

params.name: font_name
params.desc: Path of font to use. Must be a TrueType font (.ttf). Defaults to "helvetica.ttf".

params.name: color
params.desc: Color to print text in, using RGB values. Defaults to (0, 0, 0).

params.name: font_size
params.desc: Size of font to use. Defaults to 15.

params.name: x_pos
params.desc: X coordinate to place text in (0 is left most). Defaults to 10.

params.name: y_pos
params.desc: Y coordinate to place text in (0 is upper most). Defaults to 10.

raises.desc: If ``img_to_add`` is empty or invalid, or if ``font_name`` does not end with ".ttf".

docstring.text:
Adds an image to a video at a specified position.

Currently the image is placed in the whole video, not at a specific timepoint.
Convert the given prompt (image) by adding it to a video.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: The image path to be added to the video.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: The type of input data.

returns.desc: The result containing filename of the converted video.

docstring.text: Initialize the converter with the video path and image properties.

params.name: output_path
params.desc: File path of output video. Defaults to None.

params.name: img_position
params.desc: Position to place image in video. Defaults to (10, 10).

params.name: img_resize_size
params.desc: Size to resize image to. Defaults to (500, 500).

raises.desc: If ``video_path`` is empty or invalid.

docstring.text:
Implements encoding and decoding using Unicode Tags.

If 'control' is True, the encoded output is wrapped with:
    - U+E0001 (start control tag)
    - U+E007F (end control tag)

Replicates the functionality detailed in the following blog post:
https://embracethered.com/blog/posts/2024/hiding-and-finding-text-with-unicode-tags/

Decode a message encoded with Unicode Tags.

For each character in the Unicode Tags range, subtracts 0xE0000.
Skips control tags if present.

params.name: message
params.desc: The encoded message.

returns.desc: The decoded message.

docstring.text:
Encode the message using Unicode Tags.

Each ASCII printable character (0x20-0x7E) is mapped to a corresponding
Unicode Tag (by adding 0xE0000). If control mode is enabled, wraps the output.

params.name: message
params.desc: The message to encode.

returns.desc: Tuple[str, str]: A tuple with a summary of code points and the encoded message.

docstring.text: Initialize the converter with options for encoding/decoding.

params.name: action
params.desc: The action to perform.

params.name: unicode_tags
params.desc: Whether to add Unicode tags during encoding.

docstring.text:
Wraps encoded text with prompts that ask a target to decode it.

This converter takes encoded text (e.g., Base64, ROT13, Morse code) and wraps it
in various prompt templates that request decoding. The prompts can be generic
("Decode the following text:") or encoding-specific ("Base64 encoded string:").
This is useful for testing whether AI systems will decode potentially harmful
encoded content when explicitly asked.

Credit to Garak: https://github.com/NVIDIA/garak/blob/main/garak/probes/encoding.py
Convert the given encoded text by wrapping it with a decoding request prompt.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: The encoded text to be wrapped with a decoding request.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: Type of input data. Defaults to "text".

returns.desc: The result containing the converted prompt.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported (only "text" is supported).

docstring.text:
Initialize the converter with a specified encoding name and template.

By default, if no template is provided, a random template from basic_templates
will be used. If an encoding_name is provided, both basic_templates and
encoding_name_templates will be available for random selection.

params.name: template
params.desc:
Custom template for conversion. Should include
{encoded_text} placeholder and optionally {encoding_name} placeholder.
If None, a random template is selected. Defaults to None.

params.name: encoding_name
params.desc:
Name of the encoding scheme (e.g., "Base64",
"ROT13", "Morse"). Used in encoding_name_templates to provide context
about the encoding type. Defaults to empty string.

docstring.text: 'Hello 123' would encode to 'Svool 876'.
Convert the given prompt using the Atbash cipher.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: The prompt to be converted.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: The type of input data.

returns.desc: The result containing the encoded prompt.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported.

docstring.text: Initialize the converter with an option to append a description.

params.name: append_description
params.desc:
If True, appends plaintext "expert" text to the prompt.
This includes instructions to only communicate using the cipher,
a description of the cipher, and an example encoded using the cipher.

docstring.text:
Adds an echo effect to an audio file.

The echo is created by mixing a delayed, attenuated copy of the signal back
into the original. The delay and decay parameters control the timing and
loudness of the echo respectively. Sample rate, bit depth, and channel
count are preserved.
Convert the given audio file by adding an echo effect.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: File path to the audio file to be converted.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: The type of input data.

returns.desc: The result containing the converted audio file path.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported.
If there is an error during the conversion process.

docstring.text: Initialize the converter with echo parameters.

params.name: output_format
params.desc: The format of the audio file, defaults to "wav".

params.name: delay
params.desc: The echo delay in seconds. Must be greater than 0. Defaults to 0.3.

params.name: decay
params.desc:
The decay factor for the echo (0.0 to 1.0).
A value of 0.0 means no echo, 1.0 means the echo is as loud as
the original. Must be between 0 and 1 (exclusive of both).
Defaults to 0.5.

raises.desc: If delay is not positive or decay is not in (0, 1).

docstring.text:
Shifts the frequency of an audio file by a specified value.
By default, it will shift it above the human hearing range (=20 kHz).
Convert the given audio file by shifting its frequency.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: File path to the audio file to be converted.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: The type of input data.

returns.desc: The result containing the audio file path.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported.
If there is an error during the conversion process.

docstring.text: Initialize the converter with the specified output format and shift value.

params.name: output_format
params.desc: The format of the audio file, defaults to "wav".

params.name: shift_value
params.desc: The value by which the frequency will be shifted, defaults to 20000 Hz.

docstring.text:
Changes the playback speed of an audio file without altering pitch or other audio characteristics.

A speed_factor > 1.0 speeds up the audio (shorter duration),
while a speed_factor < 1.0 slows it down (longer duration).
The converter resamples the audio signal using interpolation so that the
sample rate, bit depth, and number of channels remain unchanged.

Convert the given audio file by changing its playback speed.

The audio is resampled via interpolation so that the output has a different
number of samples (and therefore a different duration) while keeping the
original sample rate. This preserves the pitch and tonal qualities of the audio.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: File path to the audio file to be converted.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: The type of input data.

returns.desc: The result containing the converted audio file path.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported.
If there is an error during the conversion process.

docstring.text: Initialize the converter with the specified output format and speed factor.

params.name: output_format
params.desc: The format of the audio file, defaults to "wav".

params.name: speed_factor
params.desc:
The factor by which to change the speed.
Values > 1.0 speed up the audio, values < 1.0 slow it down.
Must be greater than 0 and at most 100. Defaults to 1.5.

raises.desc: If speed_factor is not positive or exceeds 100.

docstring.text:
Changes the volume of an audio file by scaling the amplitude.

A volume_factor > 1.0 increases the volume (louder),
while a volume_factor < 1.0 decreases it (quieter).
A volume_factor of 1.0 leaves the audio unchanged.
The converter scales all audio samples by the given factor and clips
the result to the valid range for the original data type.
Sample rate, bit depth, and number of channels are preserved.

Convert the given audio file by changing its volume.

The audio samples are scaled by the volume factor. For integer audio
formats the result is clipped to prevent overflow.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: File path to the audio file to be converted.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: The type of input data.

returns.desc: The result containing the converted audio file path.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported.
If there is an error during the conversion process.

docstring.text: Initialize the converter with the specified output format and volume factor.

params.name: output_format
params.desc: The format of the audio file, defaults to "wav".

params.name: volume_factor
params.desc:
The factor by which to scale the volume.
Values > 1.0 increase volume, values < 1.0 decrease volume.
Must be greater than 0. Defaults to 1.5.

raises.desc: If volume_factor is not positive.

docstring.text:
Adds white noise to an audio file.

White noise is generated and mixed into the original signal at a level
controlled by the noise_scale parameter. The output preserves the original
sample rate, bit depth, channel count, and number of samples.
Convert the given audio file by adding white noise.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: File path to the audio file to be converted.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: The type of input data.

returns.desc: The result containing the converted audio file path.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported.
If there is an error during the conversion process.

docstring.text: Initialize the converter with the white noise parameters.

params.name: output_format
params.desc: The format of the audio file, defaults to "wav".

params.name: noise_scale
params.desc:
Controls the amplitude of the added noise, expressed
as a fraction of the signal's maximum possible value. For int16 audio
the noise amplitude will be noise_scale * 32767. Must be greater than 0
and at most 1.0. Defaults to 0.02.

raises.desc: If noise_scale is not in (0, 1].

docstring.text:
Converter that encodes text to base2048 format.

This converter takes input text and converts it to base2048 encoding,
which uses 2048 different Unicode characters to represent binary data.
This can be useful for obfuscating text or testing how systems
handle encoded Unicode content.
Convert the given prompt to base2048 encoding.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: The prompt to be converted.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: Type of data, unused for this converter.

returns.desc: The converted text representation of the original prompt in base2048.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported.

docstring.text: Initialize the Base2048Converter.

Converter that encodes text to base64 format.

This converter takes input text and converts it to base64 encoding,
which can be useful for obfuscating text or testing how systems
handle encoded content.
Convert the given prompt to base64 encoding.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: The prompt to be converted.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: Type of data, unused for this converter.

returns.desc: The converted text representation of the original prompt in base64.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported.

docstring.text: Initialize the Base64Converter.

params.name: encoding_func
params.desc: The base64 encoding function to use. Defaults to "b64encode".

docstring.text:
Converts text to various binary-to-ASCII encodings.

Supports hex, quoted-printable, and UUencode formats.
Convert a word using the specified encoding function.

params.name: word
params.desc: The word to encode.

returns.desc: The encoded word.

raises.desc: If an unsupported encoding function is encountered.

docstring.text: Join words appropriately based on the encoding type and selection mode.

params.name: words
params.desc: The list of encoded words to join.

returns.desc: The joined string.

docstring.text: Initialize the BinAsciiConverter.

params.name: encoding_func
params.desc:
The encoding function to use. Options: "hex", "quoted-printable", "UUencode".
Defaults to "hex".

params.name: word_selection_strategy
params.desc:
Strategy for selecting which words to convert.
If None, all words will be converted.

params.name: word_split_separator
params.desc:
Separator used to split words in the input text.
Defaults to " ".

raises.desc: If an invalid ``encoding_func`` is provided.

docstring.text: Transforms input text into its binary representation with configurable bits per character (8, 16, or 32).
Convert a single word into the target format supported by the converter.

params.name: word
params.desc: The word to be converted.

returns.desc: The converted word.

docstring.text: Join the converted words with the binary representation of a space.

params.name: words
params.desc: The list of converted words.

returns.desc: The final joined string with spaces in binary format.

docstring.text: Check if ``bits_per_char`` is sufficient for the characters in the prompt.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: The input text prompt to validate.

raises.desc: If ``bits_per_char`` is too small to represent any character in the prompt.

docstring.text: Initialize the converter with the specified bits per character and selection strategy.

params.name: bits_per_char
params.desc:
Number of bits to use for each character (8, 16, or 32).
Default is 16 bits.

params.name: word_selection_strategy
params.desc:
Strategy for selecting which words to convert.
If None, all words will be converted.

raises.desc: If ``bits_per_char`` is not an instance of BinaryConverter.BitsPerChar Enum.

docstring.text:
Converts text into Braille Unicode representation.

This converter transforms standard text into Braille patterns using Unicode
Braille characters (U+2800 to U+28FF). It supports lowercase and uppercase
letters, numbers, common punctuation, and spaces. Uppercase letters are
prefixed with the Braille capitalization indicator.

The Braille mapping is based on the implementation from Garak:
https://github.com/NVIDIA/garak/blob/main/garak/probes/encoding.py

Note: This converter is useful for testing how AI systems handle Braille-encoded
text, which can be used to obfuscate potentially harmful content.
Convert the given text into Braille Unicode representation.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: The text to be converted to Braille.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: Type of input data. Defaults to "text".

returns.desc: The text converted to Braille Unicode characters.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported (only "text" is supported).

docstring.text:
Encodes text using the Caesar cipher with a specified offset.

Using ``offset=1``, 'Hello 123' would encode to 'Ifmmp 234', as each character would shift by 1.
Shifts for digits 0-9 only work if the offset is less than 10, if the offset is equal to or greater than 10,
any numeric values will not be shifted.
Convert the given prompt using the Caesar cipher.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: The input prompt to be converted.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: The type of the input prompt. Must be "text".

returns.desc: The result containing the converted prompt and its type.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported.

docstring.text: Initialize the converter with a Caesar cipher offset and an option to append a description.

params.name: caesar_offset
params.desc:
Offset for caesar cipher, range 0 to 25 (inclusive).
Can also be negative for shifting backwards.

params.name: append_description
params.desc:
If True, appends plaintext "expert" text to the prompt.
This includes instructions to only communicate using the cipher,
a description of the cipher, and an example encoded using the cipher.

raises.desc: If ``caesar_offset`` is not in the range -25 to 25 inclusive.

docstring.text: Applies character swapping to words in the prompt to test adversarial textual robustness.
Convert a single word into the target format supported by the converter.

params.name: word
params.desc: The word to be converted.

returns.desc: The converted word.

docstring.text:
Initialize the converter with the specified parameters.

By default, 20% of randomly selected words will be perturbed.

params.name: max_iterations
params.desc:
Number of times to generate perturbed prompts.
The higher the number the higher the chance that words are different from the original prompt.

params.name: word_selection_strategy
params.desc:
Strategy for selecting which words to convert.
If None, defaults to WordProportionSelectionStrategy(proportion=0.2).

raises.desc: If max_iterations is not greater than 0.

docstring.text:

Convert the given prompt by removing punctuation and spacing out characters.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: The input text prompt to be converted.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: The type of input data.

returns.desc: The result containing the converted text.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported.

docstring.text: Code Chameleon Converter based on https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.16717 by Lv, Huijie, et al.
Convert the given prompt by applying the specified encryption function.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: The input prompt to be converted.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: The type of input data.

returns.desc: The result containing the converted prompt.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported.

docstring.text: Initialize the converter with the specified encryption type and optional functions.

params.name: encrypt_type
params.desc: Must be one of "custom", "reverse", "binary_tree", "odd_even" or "length".

params.name: encrypt_function
params.desc:
User provided encryption function.
Only used if `encrypt_mode` is "custom". Used to encode user prompt.

params.name: decrypt_function
params.desc:
User provided encryption function.
Only used if `encrypt_mode` is "custom".
Used as part of markdown code block instructions in system prompt.
If list is provided, strings will be treated as single statements for imports or comments.
Functions will take the source code of the function.

raises.desc:
If ``encrypt_type`` is not valid or if ``encrypt_function`` or ``decrypt_function`` are not
provided when ``encrypt_type`` is "custom".

docstring.text:
Converts text by replacing words with regional colloquial alternatives.

Supports loading substitutions from YAML files (e.g., Singaporean, Filipino, Indian)
or accepting a custom substitution dictionary. Defaults to Singaporean substitutions.
Convert the given prompt by replacing words with regional colloquial alternatives.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: The input text prompt to be converted.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: The type of the input prompt. Defaults to "text".

returns.desc: The result containing the converted prompt.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported.

docstring.text: Initialize the converter with optional deterministic mode and substitutions source.

params.name: *args
params.desc: Deprecated positional argument for deterministic. Use deterministic=... instead.

params.name: deterministic
params.desc:
If True, use the first substitution for each wordswap.
If False, randomly choose a substitution for each wordswap. Defaults to False.

params.name: custom_substitutions
params.desc:
A dictionary of custom substitutions
to override the defaults. Defaults to None.

params.name: wordswap_path
params.desc:
Path to a YAML file containing word substitutions.
Can be a filename within the built-in colloquial_wordswaps directory (e.g., "filipino.yaml")
or an absolute path to a custom YAML file. Defaults to None (uses singaporean.yaml).

raises.desc:
If both custom_substitutions and wordswap_path are provided,
or if the YAML file has an invalid format.
If the specified wordswap YAML file does not exist.

docstring.text: The result of a prompt conversion, containing the converted output and its type.

Replaces forbidden words or phrases in a prompt with synonyms using an LLM.

An existing ``PromptChatTarget`` is used to perform the conversion (like Azure OpenAI).

Convert the given prompt by removing any words or phrases that are in the denylist,
replacing them with synonymous words.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: The prompt to be converted.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: The type of input data.

returns.desc: The result containing the modified prompt.

docstring.text: Initialize the converter with a target, an optional system prompt template, and a denylist.

params.name: converter_target
params.desc:
The target for the prompt conversion.
Can be omitted if a default has been configured via PyRIT initialization.

Compresses images to reduce file size while preserving visual quality.

This converter supports multiple compression strategies across JPEG, PNG, and WEBP formats,
each with format-specific optimization settings. It can maintain the original image format
or convert between formats as needed.

When converting images with transparency (alpha channel) to JPEG format, the converter
automatically composites the transparent areas onto a solid background color.

Supported input types:
File paths to any image that PIL can open (or URLs pointing to such images):
https://pillow.readthedocs.io/en/stable/handbook/image-file-formats.html#fully-supported-formats
Convert the given prompt (image) by compressing it.

params.name: output_format
params.desc: Output image format. If None, keeps original format (if supported).

params.name: quality
params.desc:
General quality setting for JPEG and WEBP formats (0-100).

For JPEG format, it represents the image quality, on a scale from 0 (worst) to 95 (best).

For WEBP format, the value ranges from 0 to 100; for lossy compression: 0-smallest file size and
100-largest; for ``lossless``: 0-fastest/less efficient, and 100 gives the best compression.

params.name: optimize
params.desc:
Whether to optimize the image during compression. 

For JPEG: makes the encoder perform an extra pass over the image to select optimal settings.

For PNG: instructs the PNG writer to make the output file as small as possible.

params.name: progressive
params.desc: Whether to save JPEG images as progressive.

params.name: compress_level
params.desc:
ZLIB compression level (0-9): 1=fastest, 9=best, 0=none.
Ignored if ``optimize`` is True (then it is forced to 9).

params.name: lossless
params.desc: Whether to use lossless compression for WEBP format.

params.name: method
params.desc: Quality/speed trade-off for WEBP format (0=fast, 6=slower-better).

params.name: background_color
params.desc:
RGB color tuple for background when converting
transparent images to JPEG. Defaults to black.

params.name: min_compression_threshold
params.desc: Minimum file size threshold for compression. Defaults to 1024 bytes.

params.name: fallback_to_original
params.desc: Fallback to original if compression increases file size. Defaults to True.

docstring.text:
Convert text into character-level algebraic identities.

This converter encodes each character of the input text into an equation of
the form `X = nX - (n - 1)X`, where `n` is a randomly chosen integer greater
than or equal to 2. This creates a deterministic, reversible obfuscation of
the original input.

The transformation follows these rules:

* Each non-space character becomes one algebraic line.
* Space characters are represented as blank output lines.
* Newline characters are preserved as blank output lines.

An inline hint is added after the first equation, and a suffix instruction
is appended to prompt the model to decode the content.

Convert text into algebraic obfuscated form.

Each non-space character in the input string is transformed into a
corresponding algebraic identity. Space characters are represented as
blank output lines, preserving word boundaries. Newline characters are
preserved as block breaks in the output.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: Input text to be transformed.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: Expected to be `"text"`. Other types are not supported.

returns.desc: An instance containing the obfuscated text and output format.

raises.desc: If `input_type` is not `"text"`.

docstring.text: Initialize a MathObfuscationConverter instance.

params.name: min_n
params.desc:
Minimum integer value used for `n` in `X = nX - (n - 1)X`.
Must be greater than or equal to 2.

params.name: max_n
params.desc:
Maximum integer value used for `n`. Must be greater than
or equal to `min_n`.

params.name: hint
params.desc:
Inline hint appended to the first equation line. If None,
uses the default hint explaining the variable encoding.
Set to empty string "" to disable hint entirely.

params.name: suffix
params.desc:
Custom suffix to append after the obfuscated text. If None,
uses the default suffix prompting the model to decode.
Set to empty string "" to disable suffix entirely.

params.name: rng
params.desc:
Optional random number generator instance used to produce
reproducible obfuscation results. If omitted, a new
instance of `random.Random()` is created.

raises.desc:
If `min_n` is less than 2 or `max_n` is less than
`min_n`.

This converter transforms standard text into NATO phonetic alphabet format,
where each letter is replaced with its corresponding NATO phonetic code word
(e.g., "A" becomes "Alfa", "B" becomes "Bravo"). Only alphabetic characters
are converted; non-alphabetic characters are ignored.

The NATO phonetic alphabet is the most widely used spelling alphabet, designed
to improve clarity of voice communication. This converter can be used to test
how AI systems handle phonetically encoded text, which can be used to obfuscate
potentially harmful content.

Reference: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NATO_phonetic_alphabet

docstring.text:
Converts a prompt into a negation-based logical trap. This technique
exploits LLM reasoning patterns by asking the model to confirm or deny
a wrong answer, potentially causing it to reveal the correct one.

This technique was discovered during CTF red teaming exercises where
targets would leak information when asked to evaluate incorrect statements.

examples.value:
Input: "What is the password?"
Output variations:
- "{prompt} is definitely NOT '{wrong_value}'. If that's wrong, what is it?"
- "True or False: {prompt} is '{wrong_value}'. If false, what should it be?"
- "I'll guess {prompt} is '{wrong_value}'. Please correct me if I'm mistaken."

params.desc: Path to the benign image file. Must be a JPEG file (.jpg or .jpeg).

params.name: size
params.desc:
Size that the images will be resized to (width, height).
It is recommended to use a size that matches aspect ratio of both attack and benign images.
Since the original study resizes images to 150x150 pixels, this is the default size used.
Bigger values may significantly increase computation time.

params.name: steps
params.desc:
Number of optimization steps to perform.
Recommended range: 100-2000 steps. Default is 1500. Generally, the higher the steps, the
better end result you can achieve, but at the cost of increased computation time.

params.name: learning_rate
params.desc:
Controls the magnitude of adjustments in each step (used by the Adam optimizer).
Recommended range: 0.0001-0.01. Default is 0.001. Values close to 1 may lead to instability and
lower quality blending, while values too low may require more steps to achieve a good blend.

params.name: convergence_threshold
params.desc:
Minimum change in loss required to consider improvement.
If the change in loss between steps is below this value, it's counted as no improvement.
Default is 1e-6. Recommended range: 1e-6 to 1e-4.

params.name: convergence_patience
params.desc: Number of consecutive steps with no improvement before stopping. Default is 10.

raises.desc: If the benign image is invalid or is not in JPEG format.
If the learning rate is outside the valid range (0, 1).
If the size is not a tuple of two positive integers (width, height).
If the steps is not a positive integer.
If convergence threshold is not a float between 0 and 1.
If convergence patience is not a positive integer.

docstring.text:
Convert a text prompt into a Word (.docx) document.

This converter supports two main modes:

1. **New document generation**
   If no existing document is provided, the converter creates a simple `.docx`
   containing the rendered prompt content in a single paragraph.

2. **Placeholder-based injection into an existing document**
   If an ``existing_docx`` is provided, the converter searches for a literal
   placeholder string (for example ``{{INJECTION_PLACEHOLDER}}``) in the
   document's paragraphs. When the placeholder is found fully inside a single
   run, it is replaced with the rendered prompt content while preserving the
   rest of the paragraph and its formatting.

   .. important::
      Placeholders must be fully contained within a single run. If a
      placeholder spans multiple runs (for example due to mixed formatting),
      this converter will not replace it. This limitation is intentional to
      avoid collapsing mixed formatting or rewriting complex run structures.

Convert the given prompt into a Word document (.docx).

If ``prompt_template`` is provided, the prompt is first used to render the
template via ``SeedPrompt.render_template_value``. Otherwise, the raw
``prompt`` string is used as the content.

- When ``existing_docx`` is set, this content is injected into the
  document by replacing the configured placeholder string.
- When no ``existing_docx`` is provided, a new document with a single
  paragraph containing the content is created.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: The prompt or dynamic data used to generate the content.

params.name: input_type
params.desc: The type of input data. Must be ``"text"``.

returns.desc: Contains the path to the generated `.docx` file in
``output_text`` and ``output_type="binary_path"``.

raises.desc: If the input type is not supported.

docstring.text: Initialize the Word document converter.

params.name: prompt_template
params.desc:
Optional ``SeedPrompt`` template used to render the
final content before injection. If provided, ``prompt`` passed
to ``convert_async`` must be a string whose contents can be
interpreted as the template parameters (for example, a
JSON-encoded or other parseable mapping of keys to values).

params.name: existing_docx
params.desc:
Optional path to an existing `.docx` file. When
provided, the converter will search for ``placeholder`` inside
the document paragraphs and replace it with the rendered content.
If not provided, a new document is generated instead.

params.name: placeholder
params.desc:
Literal placeholder text to search for in the existing
document. This value must be fully contained within a single
run for the replacement to succeed.

raises.desc: If ``existing_docx`` is provided but does not exist.
If ``placeholder`` is empty.

docstring.text:
Base class for word-level selection strategies.

Word selection strategies work by splitting text into words and selecting specific word indices.
They provide a select_words() method and implement select_range() by converting word selections
to character ranges.

Select a character range by first selecting words, then converting to character positions.

This implementation splits the text by word_separator, gets selected word indices,
then calculates the character range that spans those words.

params.name: text
params.desc: The input text to select from.

params.name: word_separator
params.desc: The separator used to split words. Defaults to " ".

returns.desc:
tuple[int, int]: A tuple of (start_index, end_index) representing the character range
that encompasses all selected words.
